message:
fix(security): harden input validation with NaN/Infinity guards, saturating arithmetic, and localhost SSRF blocking

Four targeted hardening fixes across connector, runtime, and security modules:

diagnostic_registry.rs:
- Add is_finite() checks on budget_units, storm_threshold, regime_multiplier,
  and all weight fields in VoiConfig::validate() to reject NaN/Infinity inputs
  that would silently corrupt VOI scheduling decisions
- Add non-negative weight component validation (individual weights >= 0)
- Add 6 new unit tests covering non-finite budget, non-finite storm threshold,
  invalid regime multiplier, non-finite weights, and negative weight components
- Minor formatting cleanup on multi-line assert! expressions

execution_scorer.rs:
- Reorder validate_weights() to check is_finite() before sign checks,
  preventing NaN from passing the negative-weight guard
- Check weight sum is finite before comparing to zero
- Add is_finite() guard on candidate estimated_latency_ms
- Add post-computation is_finite() check on score components to catch
  overflow/NaN propagation mid-scoring, returning ScoreOverflow error
- Convert scoring map to fallible (Result) collect to propagate errors
- Add 2 new tests: invalid_weights_non_finite, non_finite_latency_rejected

obligation_channel.rs:
- Replace now_ms + default_deadline_ms with saturating_add to prevent
  u64 overflow wrapping the deadline to near-zero
- Add test_channel_deadline_saturates_on_overflow test proving saturation
  behavior at u64::MAX boundary

ssrf_policy.rs:
- Add blocked_hostname_label() function to detect localhost and *.localhost
  hostnames (case-insensitive, trailing-dot-tolerant) as SSRF targets
- Integrate hostname blocking into is_private_ip() and check_ssrf() with
  proper allowlist bypass and audit event emission
- Add 4 new tests: private_ip_localhost_hostname_aliases,
  check_ssrf_blocks_localhost_hostname_alias,
  check_ssrf_blocks_localhost_subdomain_alias,
  allowlist_permits_localhost_hostname_alias

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
